title: My Hometown
text:
Wuxi is a city in Jiangsu Province, known for its beautiful scenery and rich culture.








Taihu Lake is one of the largest freshwater lakes in China and a symbol of Wuxi.










The Lingshan Grand Buddha is one of the tallest Buddha statues in the world.










Wuxi cuisine is famous for its slightly sweet taste. Popular dishes include spare ribs and soup dumplings.

title: Mapping the Diabetes Belt
text:
Research question. How does diagnosed diabetes prevalence vary across U.S. counties, and which socioeconomic factors — poverty, lack of health insurance, median income, and educational attainment — explain that geographic variation?
Diabetes is one of the leading chronic diseases in the United States, affecting over 37 million adults. Unlike many diseases, its distribution is strikingly non-random: a well-documented “Diabetes Belt” stretches across the Southeast, overlapping with regions of high poverty, limited healthcare access, and low educational attainment. This project uses recent CDC PLACES county-level estimates to quantify these disparities and model their structural drivers.

title: Mapping the Diabetes Belt
text:
2.1 CDC PLACES — Diabetes Prevalence (County)

soql <- "SELECT statedesc, stateabbr, locationname, locationid, data_value WHERE measureid='DIABETES' AND datavaluetypeid='AgeAdjPrv' LIMIT 50000"
url  <- paste0("https://data.cdc.gov/resource/duw2-7jbt.json?$query=",
               URLencode(soql, reserved = TRUE))

places_raw <- fromJSON(url)

places <- places_raw |>
  transmute(
    state       = statedesc,
    state_abbr  = stateabbr,
    county      = locationname,
    fips        = locationid,
    diabetes    = as.numeric(data_value)
  ) |>
  filter(!is.na(diabetes))

knitr::kable(head(places, 5),
             caption = paste0("CDC PLACES diabetes prevalence — first 5 counties (N = ",
                              nrow(places), ")"))

title: Mapping the Diabetes Belt
text:
2.2 ACS Covariates (County)

acs_vars <- c(poverty_pct      = "S1701_C03_001",   # poverty rate
              uninsured_pct    = "S2701_C05_001",   # uninsured rate
              median_income    = "S1901_C01_012",   # median household income
              hs_or_higher_pct = "S1501_C02_014")   # % age 25+ who finished HS or higher

acs <- tryCatch(
  get_acs(geography = "county", variables = acs_vars,
          year = 2021, survey = "acs5", output = "wide"),
  error = function(e) NULL
)

if (is.null(acs)) {
  base <- "https://api.census.gov/data/2021/acs/acs5/subject"
  vars <- paste0(acs_vars, "E", collapse = ",")
  resp <- GET(base, query = list(get  = paste0("NAME,", vars),
                                  `for` = "county:*",
                                  `in`  = "state:*"))
  raw  <- fromJSON(rawToChar(resp$content))
  acs <- as.data.frame(raw[-1, ], stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  names(acs) <- c("NAME", names(acs_vars), "state_fips", "county_fips")
  acs <- acs |>
    mutate(GEOID = paste0(state_fips, county_fips)) |>
    mutate(across(all_of(names(acs_vars)), as.numeric))
} else {
  acs <- acs |>
    select(GEOID, NAME,
           poverty_pct   = poverty_pctE,
           uninsured_pct = uninsured_pctE,
           median_income = median_incomeE,
           hs_or_higher_pct     = hs_or_higher_pctE)
}

title: Mapping the Diabetes Belt
text:
2.3 Merged Analytic Dataset

merged <- places |>
  inner_join(acs, by = c("fips" = "GEOID")) |>
  filter(!is.na(diabetes), !is.na(poverty_pct), !is.na(uninsured_pct),
         !is.na(median_income), !is.na(hs_or_higher_pct)) |>
  mutate(income_10k = median_income / 10000)   # rescale for readable coefficients

knitr::kable(head(merged |> select(state, county, fips, diabetes,
                                    poverty_pct, uninsured_pct, median_income, hs_or_higher_pct), 5),
             digits = 1,
             caption = paste0("Merged analytic dataset (N = ", nrow(merged), ")"))

title: Mapping the Diabetes Belt
text:
3.1 Distribution of County Diabetes Prevalence

ggplot(merged, aes(diabetes)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 50, fill = "#CB4B4B", color = "white") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = mean(merged$diabetes), linetype = 2) +
  labs(x = "Age-adjusted diabetes prevalence (%)",
       y = "Number of counties",
       title = "Distribution of County-Level Diabetes Prevalence (2020)",
       subtitle = sprintf("Mean = %.1f%%, SD = %.1f%%, Range = %.1f–%.1f%%",
                          mean(merged$diabetes), sd(merged$diabetes),
                          min(merged$diabetes), max(merged$diabetes))) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

title: Mapping the Diabetes Belt
text:
3.3 State-Level Overview Map

state_agg <- merged |>
  group_by(state_abbr, state) |>
  summarise(diabetes = mean(diabetes), n_counties = dplyr::n(), .groups = "drop")

states_poly <- tigris::states(cb = TRUE, resolution = "20m", year = 2021) |>
  filter(!STUSPS %in% c("AS","GU","MP","PR","VI")) |>
  st_transform(5070) |>                               # Albers Equal Area (continental US)
  left_join(state_agg, by = c("STUSPS" = "state_abbr"))

# Inset shift for AK / HI so they sit under continental US
shift_state <- function(sf_obj, fips, dx, dy, scale = 1) {
  s <- sf_obj[sf_obj$STATEFP == fips, ]
  ctr <- st_centroid(st_union(s))
  g <- (st_geometry(s) - ctr) * scale + ctr + c(dx, dy)
  st_geometry(s) <- st_sfc(g, crs = st_crs(sf_obj))
  rbind(sf_obj[sf_obj$STATEFP != fips, ], s)
}
states_poly <- states_poly |>
  shift_state("02", dx = 2.2e6, dy = -2.5e6, scale = 0.35) |>   # Alaska
  shift_state("15", dx = 3.8e6, dy = -1.0e6, scale = 1.1)        # Hawaii

ggplot(states_poly) +
  geom_sf(aes(fill = diabetes), color = "white", linewidth = 0.3) +
  geom_sf_text(aes(label = STUSPS), size = 2.6, color = "grey20") +
  scale_fill_distiller(palette = "YlOrRd", direction = 1,
                       name = "Diabetes\nprevalence (%)",
                       na.value = "grey90") +
  labs(title = "State Average of County Diabetes Prevalence (2020)",
       subtitle = "The Southeast 'Diabetes Belt' is clearly visible") +
  theme_void(base_size = 12) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
        legend.position = c(0.92, 0.25))

title: Mapping the Diabetes Belt
Mapping the Diabetes Belt
text:
4.1 Covariate Diagnostics

X <- merged |> select(poverty_pct, uninsured_pct, income_10k, hs_or_higher_pct)
knitr::kable(round(cor(X), 2),
             caption = "Pairwise correlations among covariates")


Pairwise correlations among covariates

0.51


hs_or_higher_pct
-0.58
-0.53

1.00




vifs <- sapply(names(X), function(v) {
  others <- setdiff(names(X), v)
  f <- lm(as.formula(paste(v, "~", paste(others, collapse = "+"))), data = X)
  1 / (1 - summary(f)$r.squared)
})
knitr::kable(data.frame(Variable = names(vifs), VIF = round(vifs, 2)),
             caption = "Variance Inflation Factors")


Variance Inflation Factors



Variable
VIF




poverty_pct

2.43


uninsured_pct
uninsured_pct
1.40


income_10k
income_10k
2.14


hs_or_higher_pct
hs_or_higher_pct
1.84





Correlations are moderate and all VIFs are below 5, so a standard multivariate regression is well-identified at county scale.

title: Mapping the Diabetes Belt
text:
4.2 Primary Model: Diabetes ~ Socioeconomic Covariates
Covariates are z-standardized so the coefficients are directly comparable — each represents the expected change in diabetes prevalence (percentage points) per one-standard-deviation increase in that predictor.

df <- merged |>
  mutate(poverty_z   = scale(poverty_pct)[,1],
         uninsured_z = scale(uninsured_pct)[,1],
         income_z    = scale(income_10k)[,1],
         hs_z        = scale(hs_or_higher_pct)[,1])

fit <- lm(diabetes ~ poverty_z + uninsured_z + income_z + hs_z, data = df)
summary(fit)


Call:
lm(formula = diabetes ~ poverty_z + uninsured_z + income_z + 
    hs_z, data = df)

Residuals:
    Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
-6.8510 -0.6458 -0.0601  0.6155  5.2882 

Coefficients:
            Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
(Intercept) 10.47960    0.01848  567.23   <2e-16 ***
poverty_z    1.12920    0.02879   39.22   <2e-16 ***
uninsured_z  0.27686    0.02190   12.64   <2e-16 ***
income_z    -0.06082    0.02703   -2.25   0.0245 *  
hs_z        -0.83134    0.02507  -33.16   <2e-16 ***
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

Residual standard error: 1.036 on 3137 degrees of freedom
Multiple R-squared:  0.7796,    Adjusted R-squared:  0.7793 
F-statistic:  2774 on 4 and 3137 DF,  p-value: < 2.2e-16

title: Mapping the Diabetes Belt
text:
4.3 Coefficient Forest Plot

coefs <- tidy(fit, conf.int = TRUE) |>
  filter(term != "(Intercept)") |>
  mutate(term = recode(term,
    poverty_z   = "Poverty rate (z)",
    uninsured_z = "Uninsured rate (z)",
    income_z    = "Median income (z)",
    hs_z        = "% HS diploma or higher (z)"))

ggplot(coefs, aes(estimate, reorder(term, estimate))) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = 2, color = "grey50") +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = conf.low, xmax = conf.high), height = 0.2) +
  geom_point(size = 3, color = "#CB4B4B") +
  labs(x = "Coefficient (percentage points per SD), 95% CI",
       y = NULL,
       title = "Socioeconomic Drivers of County Diabetes Prevalence",
       subtitle = sprintf("N = %d counties, Adjusted R² = %.3f",
                          nrow(df), summary(fit)$adj.r.squared)) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

title: Mapping the Diabetes Belt
text:
4.4 Scatter — Most Important Predictor

# Identify strongest predictor by |t-value|
best <- coefs |> mutate(abs_t = abs(estimate/std.error)) |> arrange(desc(abs_t)) |> slice(1)
best_var <- c("Poverty rate (z)"="poverty_pct",
              "Uninsured rate (z)"="uninsured_pct",
              "Median income (z)"="median_income",
              "% HS diploma or higher (z)"="hs_or_higher_pct")[best$term]

ggplot(merged, aes(.data[[best_var]], diabetes)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3, color = "#555") +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "#CB4B4B", fill = "#CB4B4B", alpha = 0.2) +
  labs(x = best$term, y = "Diabetes prevalence (%)",
       title = paste("County Diabetes Prevalence vs.", best$term),
       subtitle = "Each point is a U.S. county; red line is OLS fit") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

title: About
text:
# About

Hi, this is my website.

title: Home
text:
Hi, I’m a Biostatistics graduate student at the University of Michigan.
Welcome to my personal website!





🎂 Birthday: November 6, 1998

📍 Born in: Wuxi, Jiangsu, China

🎓 Major: Biostatistics (M.S.)

💡 Interests: Statistical computing, machine learning, data analysis

🌎 Currently in: Ann Arbor, Michigan








R (tidyverse, modeling, visualization)

Python (data processing, analysis)

Statistical modeling (GLM, GEE, survival analysis)

Data visualization & reporting (Quarto, ggplot2)







I grew up in Wuxi, a beautiful city near Taihu Lake in eastern China.
You can learn more about my hometown here 👉 Wuxi Page
